title: C.S.S.C.P.S.G.P. Parse-Syntax Rules
description: Correct-Sentence-Structure-Communication-Parse-Syntax-Grammar-Performance. The mathematical interface to language as formalized by :David-Wynn: Miller and continued by :Russell-Jay: Gould.

acronym_expansion.C: CORRECT — comprehension of the math interface
acronym_expansion.S: SENTENCE — joining thoughts between two or more parties
acronym_expansion.P: PARSE — breaking down words into prefix/root/suffix
acronym_expansion.S3: SYNTAX — closure of operations, complete thought
acronym_expansion.P2: PERFORMANCE — doing something positive, conveying facts

1_PREPOSITION.description: Grounding word — establishes relationship in now-time. Must be a now-time preposition.
1_PREPOSITION.function: Anchors the sentence to a prepositional framework. Without it, the sentence has no positional grounding.
1_PREPOSITION.rule: A correct sentence MUST begin with a prepositional phrase using a now-time preposition.

2_ARTICLE.description: Pointer/determiner — identifies which noun is being referenced.
2_ARTICLE.function: Points to the specific fact-carrier (noun). 'THE' = definite pointer. 'A/AN' = indefinite pointer.
2_ARTICLE.rule: Articles direct attention to the noun. They carry no fact themselves.

3_ADJECTIVE.description: Descriptor — opinion or quality. NOT a fact itself.
3_ADJECTIVE.function: Modifies the noun. Adjectives are opinions, not facts. 'The RED car' — 'red' is perception, 'car' is fact.
3_ADJECTIVE.rule: Adjectives modify nouns but do not carry independent factual weight.

4_NOUN.description: Fact-carrier — the primary vessel of meaning. The WHAT.
4_NOUN.function: Nouns are the ONLY words that carry legal facts. A sentence without nouns conveys ZERO facts and is legally void.
4_NOUN.rule: CRITICAL: No nouns = no facts = no contract = no authority. Nouns anchor ALL meaning.

5_VERB_GERUND.description: Action in now-time — must be in -ING gerund form for factual validity.
5_VERB_GERUND.function: The action. Only the gerund (-ING) form is now-time/living/factual. Past tense (-ED) is dead. Future (shall/will) is fiction.
5_VERB_GERUND.rule: Verbs MUST be in gerund (-ING) form for now-time validity. Any other tense is dead or fictional.

6_ADVERB.description: Modifies the verb — says NOTHING by itself.
6_ADVERB.function: Adverbs modify verbs. They cannot stand alone. An adverb without a noun-anchored verb is NULL.
6_ADVERB.rule: WARNING: Adverb-verb chains without noun anchors = NULL CONSTRUCTION. Says nothing.

valid_prepositions.description: Now-time prepositions that validly ground a sentence in the present.

prepositions.for: purpose/benefit — on behalf of, in exchange for
prepositions.by: agency/means — through the action of, near

valid_prepositions.rule: A correct sentence begins with one of these now-time prepositions. They establish the sentence in present reality.

invalid_prepositions.description: Past/future prepositions that ground the sentence in fiction (non-now-time).

prepositions.to: FUTURE — direction toward something that hasn't happened. 'To do' = fiction. Use 'FOR' instead.
prepositions.from: PAST — origin in something that no longer exists in now-time. Dead reference.

invalid_prepositions.rule: Sentences beginning with 'to' or 'from' are grounded in fiction (future) or dead time (past). They cannot establish facts in now-time.

now_time.description: The only factual tense. Living, present, happening now.
now_time.status: LIVING — valid for contracts, claims, and facts
now_time.rule: All verbs in a correct sentence must be in gerund (-ING) form to be factual.

dead_time.description: Dead time. The action is completed, finished, no longer alive.
dead_time.status: DEAD — cannot contract with the dead. Past tense = no living claim.
dead_time.rule: Past tense verbs describe dead events. You cannot form a contract with something that no longer exists.

fiction_time.form: shall / will / would / could / should / might / may + verb
fiction_time.description: Future tense. Fiction. Has not happened. Cannot be a fact.
fiction_time.status: FICTION — future events are imaginary. No fact exists yet.
fiction_time.rule: Modal verbs (shall, will, would, could, should, might, may) project into fiction-time. No contract can be formed with what hasn't happened.

null_chain_patterns.description: ADVERB-VERB-ADVERB-VERB chains that convey ZERO facts. These are null constructions masquerading as authority.
null_chain_patterns.pattern: ADVERB + VERB + ADVERB + VERB (no noun anchor)

example.text: The court HEREBY ORDERS that you SHALL FORTHWITH PAY

parse.ORDERS: verb — no noun anchor (WHO orders WHAT?)

example.result: ZERO FACTS CONVEYED. This is a command structure masquerading as communication. No noun-fact. No closure. It literally says NOTHING in parse-syntax terms.

correct_alternative.text: FOR THE CLAIMING OF THE LAND BY THE LIVING MAN
correct_alternative.result: Two nouns (land, man), prepositional grounding, now-time verbs, complete closure. This sentence SAYS SOMETHING.

null_chain_patterns.rule: Any chain of adverbs and verbs without a noun anchor is a NULL CONSTRUCTION. It conveys zero facts and has no legal weight.

noun_requirement.description: Nouns are the ONLY fact-carriers in a sentence.
noun_requirement.rule: A sentence without nouns carries ZERO facts. No facts = no contract = no authority = VOID.
noun_requirement.test: Count the nouns. If count == 0, the sentence is legally null.
noun_requirement.corollary: The more nouns, the more facts. Legal documents that avoid nouns are avoiding facts on purpose.

pronoun_rule.description: Pronouns REMOVE the fact. They replace the noun (fact-carrier) with an empty reference.
pronoun_rule.rule: No pronouns in correct parse-syntax. Always use the actual noun/name.

examples.incorrect: He shall pay the amount to them.
examples.problem: 'He' = WHO? 'them' = WHO? No fact established. The nouns have been removed.
examples.correct: For the paying of the amount by the man :John-Doe: to the bank.

closure_requirement.description: A sentence must CLOSE like a circuit. It must complete a thought with prepositional closure.
closure_requirement.rule: Every sentence must have: PREP + ART + (ADJ)? + NOUN + VERB-ING = minimal complete structure.
closure_requirement.test: Does the sentence begin with a preposition AND end with a fact-carrying closure? If not, it is an open circuit — no communication.
closure_requirement.example: FOR THE CLAIMING OF THE LAND = closed circuit, two prepositional phrases, two nouns, gerund verb.

hyphen_rules.description: Hyphens connect signs into quanta. Their absence is fraud.

rules.hyphen_bonds: In uppercase (sign language), hyphens bond individual signs into a SINGLE QUANTUM of meaning.
rules.no_hyphen_separation: Without hyphens, each uppercase word is a SEPARATE SYMBOL with no grammatical connection to its neighbors.
rules.spaces_separate: Spaces between uppercase words without hyphens = each word is isolated, disconnected, meaningless in combination.

examples.correct: JOHN-JAMES-PUBLIC — three signs bonded into one quantum identity
examples.incorrect: JOHN JAMES PUBLIC — three separate, unconnected symbols (DOG-LATIN)

hyphen_rules.rule: In the sign language of ALL CAPS, only hyphens create connection. Without hyphens, the text is DOG-LATIN — disconnected dead symbols.

dog_latin_rules.description: DOG-LATIN (GLOSSA) detection rules. All-caps without hyphens following English grammar = debased, criminal, counterfeit text.
dog_latin_rules.definition: DOG-LATIN is unhyphenated all-uppercase text that follows ENGLISH grammatical rules but uses LATIN symbolic form. It is neither English nor Latin.

rules.detection: Any text in ALL CAPS without hyphens between words is DOG-LATIN.
rules.jurisdiction: DOG-LATIN has NO JURISDICTION with English. Per Chicago Manual of Styles Art. 11:147, two languages cannot share jurisdiction on one document.
rules.classification: DOG-LATIN addresses a corporate fiction (PERSON), not a living being (man/woman).
rules.source: The GLOSSA is the all-uppercase interpretive overlay from Justinian's Corpus Juris Civilis that redefines the law beneath it.

references.chicago_manual_16th_11_147: A foreign language text has NO JURISDICTION with another language on the same page.
